qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing HTTP server timeout configuration enables Slowloris DoS attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes missing HTTP server timeout configuration and Slowloris-style resource exhaustion, not the nil SuciSupiMap conversion in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap that crashes AUSF UE authentication.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Weak PRNG used for EAP-AKA' session identifier generation enables session hijacking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes predictable EAP-AKA' identifiers generated with math/rand, not the advisory's improper nil check and interface conversion in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: the free5GC AUSF UE authentication panic caused by a nil SuciSupiMap interface conversion in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap. The qualifying findings are unrelated open-world issues.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-4jrw-92fg-4jwx describes a crafted /nausf-auth/v1/ue-authentications request causing a panic in GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap when interface{} is nil instead of *context.SuciSupiMap.
The missing_http_timeouts finding reports http.Server timeout omissions at internal/sbi/server.go:53 causing Slowloris-style resource exhaustion.
The weak_prng_eap_identifier finding reports math/rand timestamp-seeded EAP identifier generation at internal/sbi/processor/ue_authentication.go:398-401.
Searches across the qualifying finding reports did not identify GetSupiFromSuciSupiMap, SuciSupiMap, or the advisory's nil interface-conversion panic.
blockers_to_higher_tier: Tier assignment is not applicable because no qualifying finding matches the target advisory root cause.
